headline: What to Know About the Air Force Refueling Tanker That Crashed in Iraq
summary: The U.S. Air Force tanker involved in the crash was a decades-old KC-135 Stratotanker built by Boeing and powered by engines from CFM International.

headline: Palantir Alliances Embed AI In Defense And Energy As Valuation Stretches
summary: Palantir Technologies (NasdaqGS:PLTR) has unveiled a cluster of new alliances across defense, aerospace, AI infrastructure, and industrial intelligence. Recent agreements include partnerships with GE Aerospace, Ondas, World View, NVIDIA, and Centrus Energy to embed Palantir’s AI platforms into mission-critical operations. The collaborations span military aviation, drone and stratospheric intelligence, sovereign AI datacenters, and nuclear fuel and energy supply chains. Palantir, long known...

headline: Huntington Ingalls Benefits From Strong Naval Shipbuilding Demand
summary: HII benefits from strong U.S. Navy shipbuilding demand and a record $53.14B backlog, though supply-chain issues and workforce shortages pose near-term challenges.

headline: How The Narrative On GE HealthCare Technologies (GEHC) Is Shifting With Mixed Analyst Sentiment
summary: The model fair value for GE HealthCare Technologies edges slightly lower from US$93.25 to US$93.05, a modest US$0.20 trim that mirrors recent shifts in published price targets. This small adjustment reflects a mixed backdrop, where some firms are lifting targets into the high US$90s, while others are cutting into the low to mid US$80s, leaving the overall narrative more finely balanced. As you read on, you will see how these differing views fit together and what to watch as the story...

headline: How New Mission‑Critical AI Partnerships At Palantir Technologies (PLTR) Have Changed Its Investment Story
summary: In recent days, GE Aerospace, Centrus Energy, Ondas, World View, LG CNS and others have announced expanded multi‑year collaborations that deepen their use of Palantir’s AI platforms across defense, energy, manufacturing, and prediction markets, while Palantir and NVIDIA unveiled a turnkey sovereign AI data center architecture. Together, these deals position Palantir’s software as a core operational layer in critical infrastructure and defense workflows, extending its reach from battlefield...
